title: Ensure reproducible simulation runs
description: Fix and record random seeds and control parallel reduction order so a run reproduces exactly.
body: Use when a simulation uses randomness or parallelism and results must be reproducible. Do not rely on a default unseeded generator, since that makes failures impossible to reproduce. Actions: set and log an explicit seed, control the reduction order in parallel sections, and verify two runs at the same seed produce identical output. Validation signals include bit-identical or tolerance-identical results across repeated runs and a recorded seed for every result. Risk boundary: nondeterministic results undermine debugging and validation, so treat a run whose seed is not recorded as unreproducible and reject it for regression use.
